title: Qan Gindoch
content:

{{Quote|Some men never learn — Qan Gindoch is one such man. I guess it was too much to expect that he might have been able to find honest work somewhere in the galaxy. Old habits die hard.|[[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]]|<ref name="WBC" />}}
'''Qan Gindoch''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] who made his living as a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Biography==
Qan Gindoch was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] in [[28 BBY/Legends|28 BBY]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tralfin]]. Working as a [[Spice/Legends|spice]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]], he was employed by the [[Givin/Legends|Givin]] [[Forger (profession)/Legends|counterfeiter]] [[Pendor Gyrr]]. Gindoch was captured by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] agents during a raid on the planet [[Mantooine/Legends|Mantooine]], and was found with a large amount of counterfeit [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] on him, which had been paid to the smuggler by Gyrr. Gindoch cooperated with the New Republic, giving testimony about much of Gyrr's counterfeiting operation. The New Republic was satisfied that Gindoch was not part of Gyrr's counterfeiting operation, and was just a smuggler employed by the Givin. As a result, and given the minor severity of his crime, Gindoch was released after paying a small fine.<ref name="WBC" />

Gindoch returned to smuggling, and began working directly for both the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and other criminal organizations. He was implicated in the [[Thief/Legends|theft]] of a shipment of [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] bound for the New Republic [[Vessitoar training facilities|training facilities]] on the planet [[Vessitoar]]. A portion of the shipment was recovered during a routine security check at a [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] on [[Celanon/Legends|Celanon]], traced to a small time [[Arms dealer/Legends|arms dealer]] who had obtained the samples from the [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Rethorn]]. Interrogation of Rethorn's agents revealed that Gindoch had smuggled the weapons aboard a stolen [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]]. The New Republic posted a reward of 3,500 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for Gindoch's capture on charges of smuggling and [[Thief/Legends|theft]].<ref name="WBC" />

==Personality and traits==
Qan Gindoch was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was 1.75 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall and weighed eighty [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]]. He had a large earring in his right ear. Gindoch was adept at bargaining with, and conning individuals, and was known to enjoy [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]].<ref name="WBC" />

==Skills and abilities==
Qan Gindoch was skilled in the use of [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] and [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee]] combat, and was trained in survival and stealth techniques. He was knowledgeable about other cultures and languages, and knew how to contact the criminal elements. Gindoch was capable of [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] [[Transport/Legends|transports]] through both [[Realspace/Legends|realspace]] and [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], and was also skilled in the use of the ship's weapons and in repairing them.<ref name="WBC" />

==Equipment==
Gindoch was armed with a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]].<ref name="WBC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Qan Gindoch was profiled in [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] a sourcebook published in [[1993]] by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. His illustration was provided by [[Mike Jackson]]. Gindoch's prison number contains a reference to THX-1138.

==Sources==
